skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, edit, and automate PowerPoint presentations with Python, including slides, charts, tables, images, templates, and bulk generation.
skill.summary: Automate PowerPoint deck creation and editing with python-pptx helpers and examples.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high findings, but inspection shows the external-command and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown fences, python-pptx references, aspect-ratio text, and dictionary key handling. The remaining real risks are expected for this skill: filesystem reads and writes for presentations and images, plus a documented API-fetch example without validation, timeout, or allowlist guidance.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated API Fetch Example
medium_findings.description: The bulk generation guide includes requests.get(api_url) and a usage example with a URL parameter. This is legitimate for API-driven reports, but users could adapt it to fetch untrusted or internal URLs without timeout, allowlist, or error handling controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network call is explicit and accepts a caller-provided api_url. The context is a documentation example rather than hidden behavior, so the risk is real but not malicious.

medium_findings.title: Presentation and Image File Writes
medium_findings.description: The helper script and examples write .pptx, optimized images, output directories, and extracted text files. This is expected for a PowerPoint automation skill, but it can overwrite files or place generated content in unintended locations if paths are not reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of file creation and saving operations. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the concern is operational safety rather than suspicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Alerts
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced code blocks and normal installation snippets, not executable Ruby backtick calls in the skill runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspected locations show Markdown code fences and standard Python import examples. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution or shell command construction in these cited locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto and Credential Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto and credential alerts map to ordinary PPTX documentation, aspect-ratio guidance, chart type validation, and dictionary .keys() calls. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, certificate handling, or credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain presentation metadata, 16:9 and 4:3 display ratios, chart type names, and list(...keys()). These contexts do not implement cryptography or credential access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request from User-Supplied URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The API generation example fetches data from an api_url parameter. Add timeout, domain validation, and error handling before using this pattern with untrusted input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The call is visible and parameterized. The example is not malicious, but the pattern can become SSRF or hang risk in automated environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Output Written to Caller Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper saves presentations and optimized images to paths derived from function arguments. Callers should use workspace-scoped output paths and review overwrite behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is direct and intentional. It is normal for this skill but still requires path care in shared or sensitive workspaces.

content.user_title: Create PowerPoint Decks with Python Automation
content.value_statement: Manual deck creation is slow when reports need consistent slides, charts, and formatting. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate and edit .pptx files with reusable Python patterns.
actual_capabilities: Create new PowerPoint presentations with title, content, and conclusion slides.
Add formatted text boxes, bullets, tables, images, and common chart types.
Edit existing decks by changing text, extracting text, reordering slides, and duplicating slides.
Apply reusable colors, fonts, layouts, and simple brand styling patterns.
Generate multiple slides or decks from CSV, JSON, spreadsheet, or API data.
Use helper functions for saving presentations, optimizing images, and adding page numbers.
limitations: Requires Python and the python-pptx package in the execution environment.
Advanced PowerPoint features can require manual XML work or PowerPoint validation.
Generated decks still need human review for layout quality and brand accuracy.
Network examples are documentation patterns and need safety controls before production use.

use_cases.title: Build recurring business reports
use_cases.description: Generate monthly or quarterly decks with consistent agenda slides, KPI summaries, tables, and charts.

use_cases.title: Automate training materials
use_cases.description: Create repeatable slide decks from outlines, lesson data, product screenshots, and branded templates.

use_cases.title: Prepare data-driven presentations
use_cases.description: Turn CSV, JSON, spreadsheet, or API data into presentation slides with visual summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PowerPoint deck about [topic] with a title slide, agenda, three content slides, and a closing slide. Use a professional style and save it as [filename].pptx.

prompt_templates.title: Add charts and tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PowerPoint report from this data: [paste data]. Include one summary slide, one bar chart, one table, and clear speaker-friendly slide titles.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open [presentation].pptx, update the title and footer text, replace outdated metrics with [new metrics], add page numbers, and save a revised copy.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk-generate personalized decks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use [csv_or_json_file] to generate one personalized PowerPoint deck per record. Keep all output in [output_directory], use the approved template, and summarize any skipped rows.

output_examples.input: A request for a five-slide Q4 review deck with revenue data and an agenda.
output_examples.output: A .pptx presentation with a title slide, agenda, executive summary, revenue chart, and next steps slide.

output_examples.input: A spreadsheet of regional sales metrics and a request for one slide per region.
output_examples.output: A generated deck where each region has a consistent slide layout, metric table, and chart summary.

output_examples.input: An existing deck that needs updated branding, page numbers, and extracted slide text.
output_examples.output: A revised presentation plus a plain text extraction file for review and editing.

best_practices: Keep source data, templates, images, and generated decks inside a project workspace.
Review generated slides in PowerPoint or compatible software before sharing externally.
Use timeouts and trusted domains when adapting the API data example.
anti_patterns: Do not run API examples against untrusted URLs without validation and timeout controls.
Do not overwrite original presentations without first saving a separate revised copy.
Do not paste sensitive customer data into generated decks unless the workspace is approved.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates and edits .pptx presentations using Python patterns and helper functions.

faq.question: Does it require Microsoft PowerPoint?
faq.answer: The examples use python-pptx, so generation can run without PowerPoint. Final visual review in PowerPoint is still recommended.

faq.question: Can it use existing templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for loading template files and applying consistent layouts, colors, and themes.

faq.question: Can it generate charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common bar, column, line, pie, and area chart workflows with formatted data.

faq.question: Can it process many presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show bulk generation from CSV, JSON, spreadsheet, and API data sources.

faq.question: What security care is needed?
faq.answer: Review output paths, protect sensitive deck contents, and add validation before using external API URLs.
